title: Bengaluru Palace
tagline: Bengaluru Palace — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Bengaluru Palace is a 19th-century royal palace located in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Bengaluru Palace is a 19th-century royal palace located in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. It was commissioned by the Maharaja of Mysore, and was completed in 1874. The palace is open for public viewing, and in the recent years, the palace grounds have been to host various events and fairs, and is home to an amusement park.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jayamahal Palace Hotel
topPlaces.description: Jayamahal Palace was earlier known as Arni Hall and originally owned by the Jagirdar of Arni in Tamil Nadu around 1892. Jayamahal Palace Hotel is a 4-star heritage luxury hotel that offers a pleasurable stay for its guests.

topPlaces.name: Anugraha
topPlaces.description: Anugraha is the official residence of the Chief Minister of Karnataka. It is located on Kumarakrupa Road, in its capital city Bangalore, and occupies 60 squares adjacent to the Chief Minister's home office.

topPlaces.name: Cunningham Road
topPlaces.description: Cunningham Road is one of the busiest commercial streets of Bangalore, the state capital of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Vasanthnagar area of the city.

topPlaces.name: Shivajinagar, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Shivajinagar, previously known as Blackpally and Tasker Town or Dandu, is a locality in Bengaluru, India located in the Central Business District. It was named after the 17th century Maratha Emperor Shivaji, by Shivaji Nagar two time independent Councillor Sri N R Murthy who was opposition leader, in the Bangalore City Corporation, in the year 1956.

topPlaces.name: National Military Memorial
topPlaces.description: National Military Memorial (Rashtriya Sainika Smaraka) is a memorial park located in Bengaluru and established by the Government of Karnataka in 2013 to honour the men and families of martyrs who died in the service of the nation.

topPlaces.name: Devara Jeevanahalli
topPlaces.description: Devara Jeevanahalli (or spelled as Devarajeevanahalli and popularly known as DJ Halli) is a locality in North Bangalore, India, adjoining Bangalore Cantonment and R. T. Nagar. The locality is part of Pulakeshinagar Assembly constituency and Bangalore North Lok Sabha constituency.

topPlaces.name: Karnataka Chitrakala Parishath
topPlaces.description: Karnataka Chitrakala Parishath (Kannada: ಕರ್ನಾಟಕ ಚಿತ್ರಕಲಾ ಪರಿಷತ್) is a visual art complex located in Bangalore. The complex has 18 galleries.

topPlaces.name: Sadashivanagar
topPlaces.description: Sadashivanagar is an affluent residential neighbourhood in the north western part of Bangalore in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is bounded by Malleshwaram to the west,Vyalikaval and Palace Guttahalli to the south, Palace grounds to the east and Hebbal to the north.

items.note: Jayamahal Palace was earlier known as Arni Hall and originally owned by the Jagirdar of Arni in Tamil Nadu around 1892. Jayamahal Palace Hotel is a 4-star heritage luxury hotel that offers a pleasurable stay for its guests.

Anugraha is the official residence of the Chief Minister of Karnataka. It is located on Kumarakrupa Road, in its capital city Bangalore, and occupies 60 squares adjacent to the Chief Minister's home office.
Cunningham Road is one of the busiest commercial streets of Bangalore, the state capital of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Vasanthnagar area of the city.

Shivajinagar, previously known as Blackpally and Tasker Town or Dandu, is a locality in Bengaluru, India located in the Central Business District. It was named after the 17th century Maratha Emperor Shivaji, by Shivaji Nagar two time independent Councillor Sri N R Murthy who was opposition leader, in the Bangalore City Corporation, in the year 1956.

National Military Memorial (Rashtriya Sainika Smaraka) is a memorial park located in Bengaluru and established by the Government of Karnataka in 2013 to honour the men and families of martyrs who died in the service of the nation.

Devara Jeevanahalli (or spelled as Devarajeevanahalli and popularly known as DJ Halli) is a locality in North Bangalore, India, adjoining Bangalore Cantonment and R. T. Nagar. The locality is part of Pulakeshinagar Assembly constituency and Bangalore North Lok Sabha constituency.
Karnataka Chitrakala Parishath (Kannada: ಕರ್ನಾಟಕ ಚಿತ್ರಕಲಾ ಪರಿಷತ್) is a visual art complex located in Bangalore. The complex has 18 galleries.

Sadashivanagar is an affluent residential neighbourhood in the north western part of Bangalore in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is bounded by Malleshwaram to the west,Vyalikaval and Palace Guttahalli to the south, Palace grounds to the east and Hebbal to the north.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bengaluru Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bengaluru Palace is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bengaluru Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (25 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bengaluru Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bengaluru Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bengaluru Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Bengaluru Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bengaluru Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jayamahal Palace Hotel, Anugraha, Cunningham Road, Shivajinagar, Bengaluru, National Military Memorial.
